---
title: Two-Factor ANOVA
subtitle: Sample Problems
# author: Guenther, Benjamin A.
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs
format: 
  html:
    html-math-method: mathjax
    self-contained-math: false
    embed-resources: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import importlib
import stats_project 
import random

# This is to clear cache and reload after edits to the .py file
importlib.reload(stats_project)
from stats_project import FactorialData
```

## About
the factorial ANOVA problems use the `FactorialData` class which requires:

* a tuple to specify the levels of each factor - (factor A, factor B)
* the sample size per group - all groups will have the same sample size

Each of the problems below sets factor levels randomly with 2 or 3 levels.  Per-group sample size is randomly set between 5 and 15. An example funciton call is included below

```{python}
#| eval: false
levels_factor_a = random.randint(2,3)
levels_factor_b = random.randint(2,3)
n = random.randint(5,15)
FactorialData(design = (levels_factor_a, levels_factor_b), group_n = n).factorial_ANOVA()
```


## Problem 1
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
levels_factor_a = random.randint(2,3)
levels_factor_b = random.randint(2,3)
n = random.randint(5,15)
FactorialData(design = (levels_factor_a, levels_factor_b), group_n = n).factorial_ANOVA()
```

## Problem 2
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
levels_factor_a = random.randint(2,3)
levels_factor_b = random.randint(2,3)
n = random.randint(5,15)
FactorialData(design = (levels_factor_a, levels_factor_b), group_n = n).factorial_ANOVA()
```

## Problem 3
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
levels_factor_a = random.randint(2,3)
levels_factor_b = random.randint(2,3)
n = random.randint(5,15)
FactorialData(design = (levels_factor_a, levels_factor_b), group_n = n).factorial_ANOVA()
```

## Problem 4
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
levels_factor_a = random.randint(2,3)
levels_factor_b = random.randint(2,3)
n = random.randint(5,15)
FactorialData(design = (levels_factor_a, levels_factor_b), group_n = n).factorial_ANOVA()
```

## Problem 5
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
levels_factor_a = random.randint(2,3)
levels_factor_b = random.randint(2,3)
n = random.randint(5,15)
FactorialData(design = (levels_factor_a, levels_factor_b), group_n = n).factorial_ANOVA()
```
